volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Antigone (Section 1.335 - Section 509)

sections.label: Section 1.335
sections.original: χωρεῖ , περιβρυχίοισιν
sections.translation: This power spans the sea , even when it surges white before the gales of the south-wind , and makes a path under swells that threaten to engulf him . Earth , too , the eldest of the gods , the immortal , the unwearied ,

sections.label: Section 1.340
sections.original: ἱππείῳ γένει πολεύων .
sections.translation: he wears away to his own ends , turning the soil with the offspring of horses as the plows weave to and fro year after year .

sections.label: Section 1.343
sections.original: κουφονόων τε φῦλον ὀρνίθων ἀμφιβαλὼν ἄγει
sections.translation: The light-hearted tribe of birds

sections.label: Section 1.345
sections.original: καὶ θηρῶν ἀγρίων ἔθνη πόντου τʼ εἰναλίαν φύσιν
sections.translation: and the clans of wild beasts and the sea-brood of the deep he snares in the meshes of his twisted nets , and he leads them captive , very-skilled man . He masters by his arts

sections.label: Section 1.350
sections.original: θηρὸς ὀρεσσιβάτα , λασιαύχενά θʼ
sections.translation: the beast who dwells in the wilds and roams the hills . He tames the shaggy-maned horse , putting the yoke upon its neck , and tames the tireless mountain bull .

sections.label: Section 2.355
sections.original: καὶ φθέγμα καὶ ἀνεμόεν φρόνημα καὶ ἀστυνόμους
sections.translation: wind and the moods that give order to a city he has taught himself , and how to flee the arrows of the inhospitable frost under clear skies and the arrows of the storming rain .

sections.label: Section 2.360
sections.original: τὸ μέλλον · Ἅιδα μόνον φεῦξιν οὐκ ἐπάξεται ·
sections.translation: He has resource for everything . Lacking resource in nothing he strides towards what must come . From Death alone he shall procure no escape , but from baffling diseases he has devised flights .

sections.label: Section 2.365
sections.original: σοφόν τι τὸ μηχανόεν τέχνας ὑπὲρ ἐλπίδʼ ἔχων
sections.translation: Possessing resourceful skill , a subtlety beyond expectation he moves now to evil , now to good . When he honors the laws of the land and the justice of the gods to which he is bound by oath ,

sections.label: Section 2.370
sections.original: ὑψίπολις · ἄπολις ὅτῳ τὸ μὴ καλὸν
sections.translation: his city prospers . But banned from his city is he who , thanks to his rashness , couples with disgrace . Never may he share my home ,

sections.label: Section 2.375
sections.original: γένοιτο μήτʼ ἴσον φρονῶν ὃς τάδʼ ἔρδει .
sections.translation: never think my thoughts , who does these things !

sections.label: Section 376
sections.original: ἐς δαιμόνιον τέρας ἀμφινοῶ
sections.translation: What marvel sent by the gods is this ?— I am bewildered ! I know her . How can I deny that this girl is Antigone ? O unhappy child

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: καὶ δυστήνου πατρὸς Οἰδιπόδα ,
sections.translation: of your unhappy father , of Oedipus ! What can this mean ? What ! Surely they are not bringing you captive for disobeying the King ’ s laws and being caught in lunacy ?

sections.label: Section 384
sections.original: ἥδʼ ἔστʼ ἐκείνη τοὔργον ἡ ʼξειργασμένη ·
sections.translation: Here is she , the one who did the deed .

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: τήνδʼ εἵλομεν θάπτουσαν . ἀλλὰ ποῦ Κρέων ;
sections.translation: We caught this one burying him . Where is Creon ?

sections.label: Section 386
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐκ δόμων ἄψορρος εἰς δέον περᾷ .
sections.translation: There , he is coming from the house again at our need .

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι ; ποίᾳ ξύμμετρος προὔβην τύχῃ ;
sections.translation: What is it ? What has happened that makes my coming timely ?

sections.label: Section 388
sections.original: ἄναξ , βροτοῖσιν οὐδέν ἔστʼ ἀπώμοτον .
sections.translation: My king , there is nothing that a man can rightly swear he will not do . For second thought belies one ’ s first intent .

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: σχολῇ ποθʼ ἥξειν δεῦρʼ ἂν ἐξηύχουν ἐγὼ
sections.translation: I could have vowed that I would not ever come here again , because of your threats by which I had just been storm-tossed . But since this joy that exceeds and oversteps my hopes can be compared in fulness to no other pleasure , I am back — though it is contrary to my sworn oath —

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: κόρην ἄγων τήνδʼ , ἣ καθῃρέθη τάφον
sections.translation: bringing this girl who was caught giving burial honors to the dead . This time there was no casting of lots . No , this piece of luck has fallen to me , and me alone . And now , my king , as it pleases you , take her yourself , question her and convict her . But justice would see me

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: δίκαιός εἰμι τῶνδʼ ἀπηλλάχθαι κακῶν .
sections.translation: released free and clear from this trouble .

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: ἄγεις δὲ τήνδε τῷ τρόπῳ πόθεν λαβών ;
sections.translation: Your prisoner here — how and where did you take her ?

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: αὕτη τὸν ἄνδρʼ ἔθαπτε · πάντʼ ἐπίστασαι .
sections.translation: She was burying the man . You know all there is to tell .

sections.label: Section 403
sections.original: ἦ καὶ ξυνίης καὶ λέγεις ὀρθῶς ἃ φῄς ;
sections.translation: Are you clear and sure about what you are saying ?

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: ταύτην γʼ ἰδὼν θάπτουσαν ὃν σὺ τὸν νεκρὸν
sections.translation: I am . I saw her burying the corpse that you

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: ἀπεῖπας . ἆρʼ ἔνδηλα καὶ σαφῆ λέγω ;
sections.translation: had forbidden to bury . Is that plain and sufficient ?

sections.label: Section 406
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ὁρᾶται κἀπίληπτος ᾑρέθη ;
sections.translation: And how was she observed ? How taken in the act ?

sections.label: Section 407
sections.original: τοιοῦτον ἦν τὸ πρᾶγμʼ . ὅπως γὰρ ἥκομεν ,
sections.translation: It happened like this . When we had come to the place with those fierce threats of yours still in our ears , we swept away all the dust that covered

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: νέκυν , μυδῶν τε σῶμα γυμνώσαντες εὖ ,
sections.translation: the corpse and bared the damp body well . We then sat down on the brow of the hill to windward , fleeing the smell from him , lest it strike us . Each man was wide awake and kept his neighbor alert with torrents of threats , if any one should be careless of this task .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: χρόνον τάδʼ ἦν τοσοῦτον , ἔστʼ ἐν αἰθέρι
sections.translation: So time passed , until the disk of the sun stood bright in mid-sky and the heat began to burn . And then suddenly a whirlwind lifted from the earth a storm of dust , a trouble in the sky , and it filled the plain , marring all the foliage of its woods .

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: ὕλης πεδιάδος , ἐν δʼ ἐμεστώθη μέγας
sections.translation: Soon the wide air was choked with it . We closed our eyes , and endured the plague from the gods . When , after a long while , this storm had passed , the girl was seen , and she wailed aloud with the sharp cry of a grieving bird , as when inside her empty

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: εὐνῆς νεοσσῶν ὀρφανὸν βλέψῃ λέχος .
sections.translation: nest she sees the bed stripped of its nestlings . So she , too , when she saw the corpse bare , broke into a cry of lamentation and cursed with harsh curses those who had done it . Immediately she took thirsty dust in her hands ,

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: ἔκ τʼ εὐκροτήτου χαλκέας ἄρδην πρόχου
sections.translation: and from a pitcher of beaten bronze held high she crowned the dead with thrice-poured libations . We rushed forward when we saw it , and at once closed upon our quarry , who was not at all dismayed . We then charged her with her past and present doings ,

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: πράξεις · ἄπαρνος δʼ οὐδενὸς καθίστατο ,
sections.translation: but she made no denial of anything — at once to my joy and to my pain . For to have escaped from trouble one ’ s self gives the greatest joy , but it stings to lead friends to evil . Naturally , though , all such things are

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: ἐμοὶ πέφυκε τῆς ἐμῆς σωτηρίας .
sections.translation: of less account to me than my own safety .

sections.label: Section 441
sections.original: σὲ δή , σὲ τὴν νεύουσαν εἰς πέδον κάρα ,
sections.translation: You , you with your face bent to the ground , do you admit , or deny that you did this ?

sections.label: Section 443
sections.original: καὶ φημὶ δρᾶσαι κοὐκ ἀπαρνοῦμαι τὸ μή .
sections.translation: I declare it and make no denial .

sections.label: Section 444
sections.original: σὺ μὲν κομίζοις ἂν σεαυτὸν ᾖ θέλεις
sections.translation: You can take yourself wherever you please ,

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ἔξω βαρείας αἰτίας ἐλεύθερον ·
sections.translation: free and clear of a heavy charge . Exit Guard .

sections.label: Section 446
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἰπέ μοι μὴ μῆκος , ἀλλὰ συντόμως ,
sections.translation: You , however , tell me — not at length , but briefly — did you know that an edict had forbidden this ?

sections.label: Section 448
sections.original: ᾔδη · τί δʼ οὐκ ἔμελλον ; ἐμφανῆ γὰρ ἦν .
sections.translation: I knew it . How could I not ? It was public .

sections.label: Section 449
sections.original: καὶ δῆτʼ ἐτόλμας τούσδʼ ὑπερβαίνειν νόμους ;
sections.translation: And even so you dared overstep that law ?

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τί μοι Ζεὺς ἦν ὁ κηρύξας τάδε ,
sections.translation: Yes , since it was not Zeus that published me that edict , and since not of that kind are the laws which Justice who dwells with the gods below established among men . Nor did I think that your decrees were of such force , that a mortal could override the unwritten

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: νόμιμα δύνασθαι θνητὸν ὄνθʼ ὑπερδραμεῖν .
sections.translation: and unfailing statutes given us by the gods . For their life is not of today or yesterday , but for all time , and no man knows when they were first put forth . Not for fear of any man ’ s pride was I about to owe a penalty to the gods for breaking these .

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: δώσειν · θανουμένη γὰρ ἐξῄδη , τί δʼ οὔ ;
sections.translation: Die I must , that I knew well ( how could I not ?). That is true even without your edicts . But if I am to die before my time , I count that a gain . When anyone lives as I do , surrounded by evils , how can he not carry off gain by dying ?

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: οὕτως ἔμοιγε τοῦδε τοῦ μόρου τυχεῖν
sections.translation: So for me to meet this doom is a grief of no account . But if I had endured that my mother ’ s son should in death lie an unburied corpse , that would have grieved me . Yet for this , I am not grieved . And if my present actions are foolish in your sight ,

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: σχεδόν τι μώρῳ μωρίαν ὀφλισκάνω .
sections.translation: it may be that it is a fool who accuses me of folly .

sections.label: Section 471
sections.original: δηλοῖ τὸ γέννημʼ ὠμὸν ἐξ ὠμοῦ πατρὸς
sections.translation: She shows herself the wild offspring of a wild father , and does not know how to bend before troubles .

sections.label: Section 473
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἴσθι τοι τὰ σκλήρʼ ἄγαν φρονήματα
sections.translation: Yet remember that over-hard spirits most often collapse . It is the stiffest iron , baked to

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: σίδηρον ὀπτὸν ἐκ πυρὸς περισκελῆ
sections.translation: utter hardness in the fire , that you most often see snapped and shivered . And I have witnessed horses with great spirit disciplined by a small bit . For there is no place for pride , when one is his neighbors ’ slave .

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: αὕτη δʼ ὑβρίζειν μὲν τότʼ ἐξηπίστατο ,
sections.translation: This girl was already practiced in outrage when she overstepped the published laws . And , that done , this now is a second outrage , that she glories in it and exults in her deed . In truth , then , I am no man , but she is ,

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: εἰ ταῦτʼ ἀνατὶ τῇδε κείσεται κράτη .
sections.translation: if this victory rests with her and brings no penalty . No ! Whether she is my sister ’ s child , or nearer to me in blood than any of my kin that worship Zeus at the altar of our house , she and her sister will not escape a doom most harsh . For in truth

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ἐπαιτιῶμαι τοῦδε βουλεῦσαι τάφου .
sections.translation: I charge that other with an equal share in the plotting of this burial . Call her out ! I saw her inside just now , raving , and not in control of her wits . Before the deed , the mind frequently is convicted of stealthy crimes when conspirators are plotting depravity in the dark .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: μισῶ γε μέντοι χὤταν ἐν κακοῖσί τις
sections.translation: But , truly , I detest it , too , when one who has been caught in treachery then seeks to make the crime a glory .

sections.label: Section 497
sections.original: θέλεις τι μεῖζον ἢ κατακτεῖναί μʼ ἑλών ;
sections.translation: What more do you want than to capture and kill me ?

sections.label: Section 498
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οὐδέν · τοῦτʼ ἔχων ἅπαντʼ ἔχω .
sections.translation: I want nothing else . Having that , I have everything .

sections.label: Section 499
sections.original: τί δῆτα μέλλεις ; ὡς ἐμοὶ τῶν σῶν λόγων
sections.translation: Why then do you wait ? In none of your maxims

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: ἀρεστὸν οὐδὲν μηδʼ ἀρεσθείη ποτέ ·
sections.translation: is there anything that pleases me — and may there never be ! Similarly to you as well my views must be displeasing . And yet , how could I have won a nobler glory than by giving burial to my own brother ? All here would admit that they approve ,

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: λέγοιτʼ ἄν , εἰ μὴ γλῶσσαν ἐγκλῄοι φόβος .
sections.translation: if fear did not grip their tongues . But tyranny , blest with so much else , has the power to do and say whatever it pleases .

sections.label: Section 508
sections.original: σὺ τοῦτο μούνη τῶνδε Καδμείων ὁρᾷς .
sections.translation: You alone out of all these Thebans see it that way .

sections.label: Section 509
sections.original: ὁρῶσι χοὖτοι , σοὶ δʼ ὑπίλλουσιν στόμα .
sections.translation: They do , too , but for you they hold their tongues .
